headline: In the West Bank, Israeli settlers who commit crimes enjoy total impunity
description: Since 2020, Israeli courts have not handed down any jail sentences to Jewish settlers found responsible for the deaths of Palestinian civilians, even as both the number and severity of the crimes they commit have continued to increase.

headline: Iran announces reopening of Strait of Hormuz, but threatens to close it again
Trump says will bring Iran uranium 'back home to the USA'
In the United Kingdom, Prime Minister Keir Starmer once again weakened by the Mandelson affair
description: According to revelations by the Guardian on Thursday, April 16, the former ambassador to the United States, implicated in the Jeffrey Epstein affair, was sent to Washington without having received the necessary security clearance. Keir Starmer said on Friday that he was stunned not to have been informed.

headline: France's 'Clair Obscur' wins BAFTA for best video game
description: Having sold more than five million copies, the first game from Sandfall Interactive, 'Clair Obscur: Expedition 33,' has become a global phenomenon since its release last year.

headline: The radical eye and many lives of photographer Lee Miller at the Musée d'art moderne de Paris
description: A retrospective rich with vintage prints traces the career of this American force who constantly reinvented herself, from surrealist artist to war correspondent.

headline: Ireland, striving to become Europe's data center hub, falls victim to its own success
description: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

Suspense and pretense: The RN prepares for life after Le Pen
description: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.

headline: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
description: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

headline: 'After Greenland and Iran, the Hungarian election marks a new step in the transatlantic rift'
description: The crushing electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, despite massive support from Washington, underscores the failure of Donald Trump's ideological campaign in Europe. The gap is widening inexorably, writes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.
